scenario_results.reason: The reporting packet was assembled with evidence categories, reporting tie-outs, and explicit unresolved dependencies.
Intake validation correctly blocked execution until mandatory program, period, and jurisdiction facts were present.
Evidence gaps were identified concretely without inventing missing reassessment artifacts.
Disclosure-risk handling applied minimum-necessary and preserved tenant privacy boundaries.
Unsupported policy exception was rejected and routed to the correct authority lane.
Low-confidence handling named missing jurisdiction and period details and requested clarification.
Clinical/privacy scope was routed out to adjacent specialists with full context preserved.
Conflicting incident and timeline data was surfaced and routed for reconciliation rather than hidden.
Unsupported certainty was not claimed where evidence remained incomplete.
Boundary drift into clinical interpretation was rejected with clear adjacent-lane routing.
Repeat-failure condition was converted into a process-level escalation artifact.
Conflicting policy sources were preserved and escalated instead of overfitting a synthesis.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed the validated standard against the defined workflow and boundary contract.
The package remains validated on paper with no retained-implementation runtime claim and explicit escalation/return conditions.
